# The Prototype Factory

Spin up a complete AI prototype in one session. This runbook provisions a GitHub repo, scaffolds a Next.js frontend, wires up authentication, orchestrator APIs, retrieval, telemetry, and CI/CD — all from a single set of inputs.

<Admonition type="info" title="Clean Contracts, Swappable Implementations" description="We optimize for replaceability. Tools will change; interfaces should not. If a decision makes migration harder, it needs a contract." />

---

## Pre-flight Checks

Before we begin, let's make sure your system has the required tools.

<Check
    id="check-gh-install"
    path="checks/check-gh.sh"
    title="GitHub CLI installed and authenticated"
    description="The `gh` CLI is required to create repos and pull requests. Install it from [cli.github.com](https://cli.github.com/)."
    successMessage="`gh` is installed and authenticated!"
    failMessage="`gh` is not installed or not authenticated. Install from https://cli.github.com/ and run `gh auth login`."
/>

<Check
    id="check-node-install"
    path="checks/check-node.sh"
    title="Node.js and npx available"
    description="Node.js is required to scaffold the Next.js frontend. Install from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/)."
    successMessage="Node.js and npx are ready!"
    failMessage="Node.js is not installed. Install from https://nodejs.org/"
/>

---

## Authenticate

### GitHub

We need GitHub access to create repos and push code.

<GitHubAuth
    id="gh-auth"
    title="Authenticate to GitHub"
    description="Connect to GitHub to create repositories and push scaffolded code."
/>

### AWS

Authenticate to AWS for infrastructure provisioning and deployment.

<AwsAuth
    id="aws-auth"
    title="Authenticate to AWS"
    description="Connect to the AWS account where prototype infrastructure will be provisioned."
    defaultRegion="us-west-2"
    ssoStartUrl="https://d-9267d384ee.awsapps.com/start"
    ssoRegion="us-west-2"
/>

---

## Configure Your Prototype

<Inputs id="prototype-config">
```yaml
variables:
  - name: ProjectName
    type: string
    description: "The name of your prototype project. Used for the GitHub repo, Supabase project, Pinecone index, and more."
    default: "my-prototype"
    validations:
      - required
  - name: ProjectDescription
    type: string
    description: "A short description for the GitHub repo."
    default: "AI-powered prototype application"
```
</Inputs>

---

## 1. Create the GitHub Repo

<Command
    id="create-github-repo"
    command="gh repo create gruntwork-test/{{ .ProjectName }} --private --description '{{ .ProjectDescription }}' && echo Repo created: https://github.com/gruntwork-test/{{ .ProjectName }}"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Create the GitHub repository"
    description="Creates a new private repository in your GitHub organization."
    successMessage="GitHub repo created successfully!"
    failMessage="Failed to create the GitHub repo. It may already exist — check GitHub."
    githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

<Check
    id="verify-repo"
    command="gh repo view gruntwork-test/{{ .ProjectName }} --json name,url --jq '.url'"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Verify repo was created"
    description="Confirms the repository exists on GitHub."
    successMessage="Repo confirmed!"
    failMessage="Could not find the repo. Please check the inputs and try again."
    githubAuthId="gh-auth"
    usePty={false}
/>

---

## 2. Clone the Repo & Scaffold the Next.js Frontend

First, clone the repo we just created. This will show the repo in the file workspace and make it available to all downstream steps.

<GitClone
    id="clone-repo"
    gitHubAuthId="gh-auth"
    title="Clone the prototype repo"
    description="Clone the repository so we can scaffold the frontend and add configuration files."
    prefilledUrl="https://github.com/gruntwork-test/{{ .ProjectName }}"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
/>

Now run `create-next-app` into the cloned repo, add Supabase Auth helpers, and push to `main`.

<Command
    id="scaffold-nextjs"
    path="scripts/scaffold-nextjs.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Scaffold Next.js application"
    description="Runs `create-next-app` with TypeScript + Tailwind, adds Supabase auth dependencies, then pushes to GitHub."
    successMessage="Next.js app scaffolded and pushed to GitHub!"
    failMessage="Failed to scaffold the Next.js app."
    githubAuthId="gh-auth"
    usePty={false}
/>

---

## 3. Configure Supabase Auth

Sets up Supabase as the identity provider with Microsoft OAuth for org-gated access. This establishes the **AuthContext abstraction** — Supabase now, Auth0 later.

<Command
    id="configure-supabase"
    path="scripts/configure-supabase-auth.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Configure Supabase Auth"
    description="Creates a Supabase project, configures Microsoft OAuth, sets up org-based access rules, and runs the initial auth schema migration."
    successMessage="Supabase Auth configured!"
    failMessage="Failed to configure Supabase Auth."
/>

---

## 4. Provision the Conversation Service API

The Conversation Service is an **orchestrator**: it takes a user message and composes auth context → retrieval → prompt selection → model call → persistence → telemetry.

<Command
    id="configure-conversation-api"
    path="scripts/configure-conversation-api.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Provision Conversation Service API"
    description="Scaffolds a FastAPI application for the conversation orchestrator, configures the LLM provider, and initializes the prompt registry."
    successMessage="Conversation Service API provisioned!"
    failMessage="Failed to provision the Conversation Service API."
/>

---

## 5. Provision the Content Service API

The Content Service owns **canonical content addressing** — stable locators that survive tool changes, so every citation can be re-opened in full context.

<Command
    id="configure-content-api"
    path="scripts/configure-content-api.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Provision Content Service API"
    description="Scaffolds a FastAPI application for the content service with canonical citation schema and deep-link support."
    successMessage="Content Service API provisioned!"
    failMessage="Failed to provision the Content Service API."
/>

---

## 6. Provision the Vector Database

Sets up Pinecone for retrieval, behind a clean **RetrievalProvider interface** that can be swapped to Databricks Vector Search in production.

<Command
    id="provision-vector-db"
    path="scripts/provision-vector-db.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Provision vector database"
    description="Creates a Pinecone index, configures the retrieval interface, and sets up metadata filtering for teacher/collection/topic."
    successMessage="Vector database provisioned!"
    failMessage="Failed to provision the vector database."
/>

---

## 7. Configure Cache & Rate Limiting

Protects the system from bursts and speeds up repeat retrieval with Upstash Redis.

<Command
    id="configure-cache"
    path="scripts/configure-cache-rate-limiting.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Configure cache and rate limiting"
    description="Provisions an Upstash Redis instance, configures per-user and per-org rate limits, and sets up retrieval result caching."
    successMessage="Cache and rate limiting configured!"
    failMessage="Failed to configure cache and rate limiting."
/>

---

## 8. Configure Telemetry & Traces

The **learning loop infrastructure**: traces, token counts, latency, retrieval hits, user feedback, and eval outcomes. No silent changes — every change shows up in trace + experiment metadata.

<Command
    id="configure-telemetry"
    path="scripts/configure-telemetry.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Configure telemetry and traces"
    description="Creates a LangSmith project, configures the trace schema, sets up feedback capture with tags, initializes the golden-set evaluation framework, and configures log redaction."
    successMessage="Telemetry and traces configured!"
    failMessage="Failed to configure telemetry."
/>

---

## 9. Generate Configuration Files

This template generates all the configuration files for your prototype stack — environment variables, service registry, and CI/CD pipeline — using values from the steps above. Review the generated service config and adjust providers or keys as needed.

<Template
    id="prototype-config-files"
    path="templates/prototype-config"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    target="worktree"
/>

---

## 10. Create Pull Request

Clone the repo, add all generated files to a feature branch, and open a pull request.

<Command
    id="create-pr"
    path="scripts/create-pr.sh"
    inputsId="prototype-config"
    title="Create pull request with generated files"
    description="Clones the repo, creates a feature branch, adds all generated configuration files (CI/CD pipeline, etc.), and opens a pull request."
    successMessage="Pull request created!"
    failMessage="Failed to create the pull request."
    githubAuthId="gh-auth"
/>

---

## Summary

You just provisioned a complete prototype stack in one session:

| Component | Status | Swap Target |
|-----------|--------|-------------|
| **GitHub Repo** | Live | — |
| **Next.js Frontend** | Live (scaffolded + pushed) | — |
| **Supabase Auth** | Configured (Microsoft OAuth + org gating) | Auth0 + RBAC |
| **Conversation Service API** | Configured (FastAPI orchestrator) | Lambdas / ECS |
| **Content Service API** | Configured (canonical citations) | TS Lambdas Reader API |
| **Vector Database** | Configured (Pinecone) | Databricks Vector Search |
| **Cache & Rate Limiting** | Configured (Upstash Redis) | CloudFront + ElastiCache |
| **Telemetry & Traces** | Configured (LangSmith) | Databricks trace events |
| **CI/CD** | Generated (GitHub Actions) | — |

### Architecture Principles Enforced

- **Clean contracts, swappable implementations** — every service has an abstract interface.
- **Canonical citations** — `work_id + locator + snippet + context_window` schema from day one.
- **Measurable learning loop** — prompt versioning, experiment pinning, trace schema, feedback tags.
- **Secure by default** — org-domain gating, rate limiting, log redaction.

### Next Steps

1. Add your team's content to the vector database
2. Configure your LLM API keys as GitHub Secrets
3. Curate your first golden-set evaluation examples
4. Start building the chat UI in the scaffolded Next.js app
